title: LATAM Healthcare Supply Chain Opacity
problem: Healthcare supply chain in LATAM is opaque and inefficient. Hospitals don't know real-time inventory levels. Stockouts of critical medications common. No visibility into supplier capacity. Manual ordering processes. No demand forecasting. COVID-19 exposed supply chain fragility with PPE and medication shortages.
solution: Healthcare supply chain management platform. Real-time inventory tracking across hospital network. Automated reordering based on par levels. Supplier marketplace with transparent pricing. Demand forecasting using AI. Integration with hospital ERPs. Analytics on utilization and costs.
technology: IoT sensors for inventory tracking, API integration with suppliers, demand forecasting ML, ERP integration, analytics dashboard
target_customer: Hospital systems, clinic networks, pharmacy chains, medical device distributors
traction_signals: Proven B2B marketplace models in other sectors
regulatory_notes: Pharmaceutical supply chain regulations, medical device distribution laws
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Supply chain infrastructure is classic B2B software. High switching costs once integrated. Recurring revenue. Similar to Procurify/Teampay but healthcare-specific.
notes: BOREALING: Supply chain software is incredibly boring. But healthcare supply chain is complex and critical. High margins once entrenched in hospital systems.
